content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

October 24, 1990

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

FPPC

OCT 24 1990

HAND DELIVERED

RE: CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE REGARDING SLATE
MAILER

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter will confirm telephone advice which
you provided to me yesterday afternoon. I phoned you
seeking advice on behalf of my client Clinton Reilly.
Mr. Reilly produces one or more slate mailers and
conducts that business through a slate mailer
organization. He files reports in accordance with the
slate mailer laws.

Mr. Reilly has become aware of a series of ballot
recommendations which have been publicly made by State
Senator Quentin Kopp. Senator Kopp's views hold sway
with certain groups of voters in the San Francisco
area. Mr. Reilly is desirous of producing a slate
mailer which would highlight Senator Kopp's
recommendations ("endorsements") to those groups of
voters. Mr. Reilly would offer to sell space on the
slate mailer to those whose ballot efforts are endorsed
by Senator Kopp.

Mr. Reilly approached Senator Kopp to assure that
the Senator would not take some adverse action against
Mr. Reilly if he published such a slate mailer. The
inquiry was one of just legal protection. There has
been no effort to coordinate, consult or cooperate with
Senator Kopp's campaign regarding the specifics of the
slate mailer.

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE I. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT P. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

Senator Kopp apparently spoke with you by
telephone on October 22 and inquired regarding this
issue. Apparently he was concerned that there be no
allegations of in-kind contributions to him from any
committee which might purchase space in such a slate
mailer. You advised there would be no problem in that
regard as long as the Senator's recommendations were in
the public domain and there was no coordination or
cooperation, etc. in the publication of the slate

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

Scott Hallabrin
October 24, 1990
Page 2

mailer. At that time, you were not aware that the slate mailer
might be published by a slate mailer organization.

I asked you about Mr. Reilly's specific circumstances, which
would involve publication of a slate mailer by a slate mailer
organization. We also discussed the application of the recent
United States District Court ruling in Wax, et al. v. FPPC (CIV-
S-90-1232). Based upon the court's ruling, which struck down
emergency regulations 18538 and 18538.2, there would be no in-
kind contribution to Senator Kopp in any event so long as the
slate mailer did not contain "express advocacy" for the election
of Senator Kopp.

Furthermore, with respect to a slate mailer produced by a
slate mailer organization, there would be neither in-kind
contributions nor independent expenditures. (Huebscher Advice
Letter No. A-89-275; Barnett Advice Letter No. I-87-258; Propper
Advice Letter No. I-86-268; and Van Winkle Advice Letters Nos. A-
90-319 and A-90-320.) This would be true even if Senator Kopp's
election was expressly advocated by the slate mailer.

However, under the Van Winkle letters, if a committee
expressly paid extra in order to cover the costs of an
endorsement of the Senator, then that would constitute either an
in-kind contribution or an independent expenditure for the
Senator. Which of these would be dependent on all the facts.

I trust that this letter accurately reflects our
conversation. If not, please advise me at once. If it does
accurately reflect our conversation, I will await your written
confirmation in the normal course of time.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

/ccz

cc: Clinton Reilly
